free_text: Excitation functions for (p,x) reactions of niobium in
the energy range of Ep=40-90 MeV
The experiment was carried out at the
Isotope Production Facility at the Los Alamos National
Laboratory using the LANSCE linear accelerator.
A stacked target design was used. A series of natural
foils of nominal thickness 25 mu-m Al foils,
25 mu-m Nb foils and 50 mu-m Cu foils were used. Six
foils of each metal with dimensions 25mm x 25mm
were used. The foils were tightly sealed into packets
using two pieces of 3M 5413-Series Kapton polyimide
film tape each piece of tape consists of 43.2 mu-m of
silicone adhesive (nominal 4.79 mg/cm2) on 25.4 mu-m
of a polyimide backing. Between each set of foils an
Al degrader was placed. On each side of target stack
a profile monitor foils was placed. Thickness and
position of individual foils is given bellow. Beam
enters the stack through 249.8 mu-m stainless steel
beam profile monitor.
Foil         Thickness  Areal density Uncertainty
SS profile monitor 249.8 mu-m     194.56      0.29
Al Degrader 01 4.96mm
Al Degrader 02 4.55mm
Al Degrader 03 3.52mm
Al Degrader 04 3.47mm
Al Degrader 05 3.46mm
SS profile monitor 124.4 mu-m     101.34      0.23
A single detector ORTEC GEM Series High-Purity
Germanium detector was used in this measurement. The
detector is a mechanically-cooled coaxial p-type HPGe
with a 1mm aluminum window, and a 49.2mm diameter,
27.9mm long crystal.
The total propagated uncertainty of the
measured activity is the quadrature sum of the
uncertainty in
-fitted peak areas,
-uncertainty in detector efficiency calibration,
-uncertainty in the gamma-ray branching ratio
data.
Compiled by S.H.
